title: Database Connection Pool Exhaustion — Checkout Service
root_cause: Connection leak in checkout-service BillingProcessor module. PreparedStatement objects in processBillingRecord() were not closed after use, causing the HikariCP pool to gradually saturate over a 75-minute window.
contributing_factors: HikariCP max-lifetime not configured (defaults to 30 min, allowing long-lived leaks)
No proactive connection leak detection alerts below 80% threshold
resolution_summary: Applied RB-DB-004 (Graceful Pool Reset). Terminated 94 idle-in-transaction connections. Deployed patch (PR#4821) to add proper finally block closing PreparedStatements.

timeline.event: RCA identifies BillingProcessor leak. RB-DB-004 approved.

lessons_learned: Implement HikariCP leak detection threshold at 30 seconds
Add connection pool alert at 70% (not 95%) for earlier warning
Mandatory code review for all PreparedStatement usage patterns
Add integration test for connection cleanup in BillingProcessor

title: Cache Stampede — Checkout Session Cache
root_cause: Simultaneous cache expiry of 50,000 checkout session keys (TTL set to flat 3600s with no jitter). All keys expired at the same second, causing a thundering herd against payments-db-v2.
contributing_factors: Missing TTL jitter in cache key creation
No circuit breaker for cache-miss fallback path
DB connection pool not sized for worst-case stampede load
resolution_summary: Applied RB-CACHE-001. Flushed expired keys. Deployed cache key creation fix with ±300s random TTL jitter.
lessons_learned: Always use TTL jitter for bulk cache operations
Add stampede-resistant cache patterns (probabilistic early expiry)
Cache hit rate monitoring with alerts below 90%

title: Auth Service Token Validation Latency Spike
root_cause: JWT validation performance regression introduced in auth-service v2.4.1. RSA key loading switched from cached to on-demand, causing 400ms overhead per request.
contributing_factors: Missing performance benchmark in CI pipeline for auth-service
No canary deployment for auth-service — full rollout pushed directly
resolution_summary: Rolled back auth-service from v2.4.1 to v2.4.0 via kubectl rollout undo. Root cause patched in v2.4.2.
lessons_learned: Add performance regression tests to auth-service CI pipeline
Use canary deployments for all auth-service updates

title: Notification Service Queue Saturation
root_cause: Runaway marketing email campaign job consumed all AMQP queue workers, blocking transactional notification delivery (order confirmations, alerts).
contributing_factors: No priority queue separation between transactional and marketing notifications
Worker pool not auto-scaled under queue depth
resolution_summary: Killed runaway campaign job. Implemented priority queue separation. Added rate limiting at 1000 campaign messages/minute.

timeline.event: Marketing campaign job started. 2M messages enqueued.
Queue backlog exceeds 500k. Transactional notifications delayed.

lessons_learned: Separate transactional and bulk notification queues
Rate limit all bulk messaging jobs
Auto-scale notification workers based on queue depth
